self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can stop routed queue linkage overstatement earlier when lifecycle sync validates concrete downstream stubs against the routing record before queue mutation.
self_improvement.mechanism: The adopted slice adds one source-side routed lifecycle-sync guard for missing and mismatched downstream stubs.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP slice, routed queue linkage mismatch could still be written through lifecycle sync and only be downgraded later by read-time validation.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Future routed queue mutations will stay closer to canonical routing truth because concrete downstream stubs must exist and match the routing record.

decision.rationale: The slice is small, source-side, and directly reduces future queue-linkage overstatement without redesigning lifecycle semantics.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
